title: World History: Year 1 to 2026
description: Important incidents and events organized by century

events.title: Birth of Christianity
events.description: Jesus of Nazareth is crucified in Jerusalem (~30-33 AD), leading to the foundation of Christianity

events.title: Eruption of Mount Vesuvius
events.description: Destroys Pompeii and Herculaneum, killing thousands. Preserved under ash.

events.title: Roman Empire reaches greatest extent under Trajan
events.description: Trajan becomes Emperor, initiating maximum territorial expansion.

events.title: Spread of Buddhism to China
events.description: Buddhist missionaries arrive in China via the Silk Road during Han Dynasty.
events.source: Hou Hanshu (Book of the Later Han)

events.title: Invention of paper in China
events.description: Cai Lun refines papermaking using mulberry bark, hemp, and rags.
events.source: Hou Hanshu (Book of the Later Han)

events.title: Five Good Emperors of Rome
events.description: Nerva, Trajan, Hadrian, Antoninus Pius, and Marcus Aurelius rule in succession, considered Rome's golden age.
events.source: Edward Gibbon, Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire; Cassius Dio

events.title: Bar Kokhba Revolt
events.description: Jewish rebellion in Judea crushed by Hadrian. Mass displacement of Jewish population, Judea renamed Syria Palaestina.

events.title: Hadrian's Wall constructed
events.description: Defensive wall across northern Britain, marking the Roman Empire's northwest frontier. 73 miles long.

events.title: Spread of Christianity throughout Roman Empire
events.description: By 200 AD, Christian communities exist in every major Roman city.

events.title: Han Dynasty begins collapse
events.description: Yellow Turban Rebellion weakens the Han Dynasty, leading to the Three Kingdoms period.
